# CDE Registry: raw-ingest-college-scorecard-institution

**Table:** `raw.college_scorecard_institution` (Bronze) with forward notes for `base.college_scorecard_institution` (Silver)
**Spec:** `docs/specs/raw-ingest-college-scorecard-institution.md`
**Ingestor:** `src/raw/college_scorecard_institution_ingestor.py`
**Date:** 2026-04-14
**Agent:** @cde-tagger
**Zone:** Bronze (raw ingest)
**Contract target:** `governance/data-contracts/raw-college-scorecard-institution.yaml` *(to be generated by @doc-generator — embed the flags in "Tag List for Data Contract" below)*

---

## Domain Context Referenced

- `governance/domain-context.md` — Higher Education Outcomes domain, College Scorecard sub-domain (lines 25–367). Establishes UNITID as the IPEDS authoritative institution identifier (auto-approve as CDE) and confirms zero PII at institutional / aggregate grain per upstream FERPA suppression.
- `governance/pii-scans/raw-ingest-college-scorecard-institution-pii-scan.md` — **Classification: NO PII.** All 28 fields (24 source + 4 metadata) are Level 1 — Public. Aggregate institution-level federal data; no individual-level records. PII flagging is therefore unanimously `false` across this contract.
- `docs/specs/raw-ingest-college-scorecard-institution.md` — Establishes that `net_price_annual` and `cost_of_attendance_annual` drive the new ROI formula, and that `CONTROL` is the switch that selects `NPT4_PUB` vs `NPT4_PRIV` (and the `NPT41..45_PUB` vs `NPT41..45_PRIV` quintile series). Glossary terms **BT-110 (Cost of Attendance), BT-111 (Net Price), BT-112 (Net Price by Income Quintile)** introduced by this spec.
- `docs/specs/roi-formula-cost-of-attendance.md` — Downstream consumer. Confirms the ROI formula rewrites to `earnings / (net_price × 4 × loan_pct)` with a fallback to `debt_median` when `net_price_annual` is null. Promotes institution-level cost from "nice to have" to "drives the stat that drives the Fight Student Loans boss."
- **Applicable regulations:** NONE in this ingest. Public federal data. FERPA protections were applied upstream by IPEDS before publication — see PII scan for the full matrix (no HIPAA, GDPR, CCPA, GLBA, SOX, or PCI exposure).
- **Sibling contract:** `governance/data-contracts/raw-college-scorecard.yaml` — same source family, same auto-approve pattern for `unitid`; six CDEs flagged there (3 grain + 3 business metrics).

---

## Table Context: Why CDE Decisions Matter Here

`raw.college_scorecard_institution` is a ~6,500-row institution-level reference table joined to the existing program-level `raw.college_scorecard` (~69,947 rows, ~2,559 institutions) on `UNITID`. It is the **cost-side** partner to the **outcomes-side** data we already have.

This table is load-bearing in a way the field-of-study file is not: it changes the business meaning of the Fight Student Loans boss and the ROI stat. With this data:

- **ROI stops being a proxy for borrowing behavior and starts being a function of what the school actually costs** × the student's own loan slider. `net_price_annual` is no longer a descriptive field — it is the **denominator input** to every ROI calculation that has an institution match. Wrong values silently mis-score ROI for every student at that institution.
- **CONTROL is not decorative** — it is the multiplexer that picks whether `npt4_pub` or `npt4_priv` is the right value for a given row. A mis-tagged `control` at a single institution produces a `net_price_annual` that comes from the wrong column, which then feeds ROI downstream. CONTROL must be right or the derivation is nonsense.
- **UNITID is the sole join key** to the existing `base.college_scorecard` and `consumable.career_outcomes` tables. There is no secondary join (no CIP, no SOC here — institution grain only). A wrong `unitid` orphans the entire row's cost data from all downstream career context.

Per Brightsmith governance policy, CDE flags do **not** propagate across zones. The Silver tagging and the Gold `consumable.career_outcomes` re-tagging will happen on their own contracts after this spec's Silver and Gold layers land.

---

## Columns Flagged as CDE — `raw.college_scorecard_institution` (Bronze)

| # | Column | Type | is_cde | Rationale |
|---|--------|------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | `unitid` | long | **true** | **The sole join key** to every downstream consumer of this table — `base.college_scorecard_institution` (Silver grain), `base.college_scorecard` (program grain, existing), and `consumable.career_outcomes` (Gold LEFT JOIN per §Zone 3 of the spec). Also the dedup grain for the Bronze ingest (one row per institution, first row wins). Auto-approved per `governance/domain-context.md` — UNITID is the authoritative IPEDS identifier. Aligned with the sibling contract `raw-college-scorecard.yaml` where `unitid` is also CDE. Without this, every cost/price field in this table is an orphan. |
| 2 | `control` | int | **true** | **The multiplexer that selects which net-price and quintile columns feed Silver.** The spec's Silver transform is literally `CASE WHEN control=1 THEN npt4_pub WHEN control IN (2,3) THEN npt4_priv END` (and the analogous expression for each of the five quintile fields). A mis-tagged `control` causes the Silver `net_price_annual` (and all five `net_price_q1..q5`) to be sourced from the wrong column, producing silently wrong ROI downstream. DQ rule enforces `control IN (1,2,3)`. External standard (IPEDS CONTROL enumeration). |
| 3 | `preddeg` | int | **true** | **Ingest filter input.** The spec filters to `PREDDEG=3` (predominantly bachelor's-granting) OR `ICLEVEL=1` (4-year institution); the 4-year-bachelor's-granting scope is the cohort this ingest exists to serve. A mis-tagged `preddeg` lets through institutions that should be filtered out, silently contaminating the row count (DQ rule 5,000–8,000) and skewing cost-of-attendance distributions. Also required to validate that the filter operated correctly when looking at any persisted row (every row in Bronze should be `preddeg=3` OR the filter matched on `ICLEVEL=1`). IPEDS external standard. |
| 4 | `costt4_a` | double | **true** | **Drives `cost_of_attendance_annual` in Silver** via `COALESCE(costt4_a, costt4_p)`. This is the "sticker price" reference number used in the ROI receipt ("Cost of attendance per year: $22,800 (sticker price)") and in the unified-COA calculation for the 4-year total `cost_of_attendance_4yr = cost_of_attendance_annual × 4`. P0 DQ rule on range ($5,000–$100,000). BT-110 (Cost of Attendance). The "A" variant is preferred because it covers academic-year programs, which are the bulk of 4-year bachelor's institutions. |
| 5 | `costt4_p` | double | **true** | **Drives `cost_of_attendance_annual` in Silver** as the `COALESCE` fallback when `costt4_a` is null (program-year institutions). Same business criticality as `costt4_a` for the subset of institutions where it is the only available COA figure. BT-110. Without this, program-year institutions would have null `cost_of_attendance_annual` and no sticker-price receipt line. |
| 6 | `npt4_pub` | double | **true** | **CRITICAL CDE.** With `npt4_priv`, this is the direct source of `net_price_annual` — the new ROI **denominator input** for public institutions (`control=1`). Every ROI calculation and every Fight Student Loans outcome for a student at a public school traces back through Silver to this column. P0 range check ($0–$60,000). DQ rule requires ≥80% non-null when `control=1` (public schools should report public net price). BT-111 (Net Price). **Highest-criticality column in the public-institution branch.** |
| 7 | `npt4_priv` | double | **true** | **CRITICAL CDE.** Same role as `npt4_pub` but for private nonprofit and private for-profit institutions (`control IN (2,3)`). P0 range check ($0–$80,000 — wider ceiling reflects private tuition reality). DQ rule requires ≥80% non-null when `control=2`. BT-111. The `control` multiplexer decides which of `npt4_pub` / `npt4_priv` is read per row; both must be CDE because either can drive the ROI denominator depending on the institution. |
| 8 | `npt41_pub` | double | **true** | **Drives `net_price_q1` in Silver** for public institutions — net price for families in the $0–$30K income bracket. Feeds the income-quintile ROI breakdown, which is how the product will eventually answer "what does this school cost a low-income student?" Also feeds the Silver DQ invariant `net_price_q1 ≤ net_price_q5` (the aid-monotonicity check — lower-income families should have lower net price due to more aid). BT-112 (Net Price by Income Quintile). |
| 9 | `npt42_pub` | double | **true** | **Drives `net_price_q2` in Silver** for public institutions ($30K–$48K income bracket). Same role as `npt41_pub`. BT-112. |
| 10 | `npt43_pub` | double | **true** | **Drives `net_price_q3` in Silver** for public institutions ($48K–$75K income bracket). Median-income bracket — the most common student profile at public institutions. BT-112. |
| 11 | `npt44_pub` | double | **true** | **Drives `net_price_q4` in Silver** for public institutions ($75K–$110K income bracket). BT-112. |
| 12 | `npt45_pub` | double | **true** | **Drives `net_price_q5` in Silver** for public institutions ($110K+ income bracket). Highest-income bracket — typically receives least aid, so this value should be the closest to `cost_of_attendance_annual` at a public institution. Part of the aid-monotonicity DQ invariant (`net_price_q1 ≤ net_price_q5`). BT-112. |
| 13 | `npt41_priv` | double | **true** | **Drives `net_price_q1` in Silver** for private institutions ($0–$30K income bracket). Private-side counterpart of `npt41_pub`. BT-112. |
| 14 | `npt42_priv` | double | **true** | **Drives `net_price_q2` in Silver** for private institutions ($30K–$48K income bracket). BT-112. |
| 15 | `npt43_priv` | double | **true** | **Drives `net_price_q3` in Silver** for private institutions ($48K–$75K income bracket). BT-112. |
| 16 | `npt44_priv` | double | **true** | **Drives `net_price_q4` in Silver** for private institutions ($75K–$110K income bracket). BT-112. |
| 17 | `npt45_priv` | double | **true** | **Drives `net_price_q5` in Silver** for private institutions ($110K+ income bracket). Private-side counterpart of `npt45_pub`; expected to be the closest to `cost_of_attendance_annual` at a private institution. BT-112. |

**Total CDE flags:** 17 of 28 columns (24 source + 4 metadata).

**Why quintile fields are CDE (response to the user's "non-CDE for quintiles?" spot-check):** The candidate list in the task prompt tentatively grouped the `npt41..45_pub/priv` series as CDE on the basis of "drives income-quintile ROI." We concur and flag all ten quintile fields as CDE. They are not merely display — the spec's Silver transform explicitly derives `net_price_q1..q5` from them, and the Silver DQ rule `net_price_q1 ≤ net_price_q5` is a P1 monotonicity invariant that relies on correct quintile values. Income-quintile ROI is a named downstream use case in the ROI-formula spec. Per BCBS 239-style reasoning, a column that is the deterministic source of a named business metric is a CDE regardless of how many quintiles exist.

---

## Columns Evaluated — Not Flagged as CDE — `raw.college_scorecard_institution` (Bronze)

| # | Column | Type | is_cde | is_pii | Reason Not Critical |
|---|--------|------|--------|--------|---------------------|
| 18 | `instnm` | string | false | false | Institution name — display label only. Aligned with the sibling `raw-college-scorecard.yaml` contract where `instnm` is also non-CDE (UNITID is the authoritative identifier). 10 names map to multiple UNITIDs (multi-campus systems) per domain context — name is not unique, so it cannot substitute as a join key. Used for receipt text and UI labels only. |
| 19 | `stabbr` | string | false | false | 2-letter state abbreviation at the institution level. Carried through Silver as `state_abbr` for filtering/display. Not a join key here (BEA RPP joins on `state_fips`, not `stabbr`; the two join happens at the Gold layer through a student-selected state, not an institution-to-RPP join on `stabbr`). Not a decision input to any downstream formula in this spec. *Could be re-evaluated in a future spec that adds state-level filters or regional analytics on top of the institution table.* |
| 20 | `tuitionfee_in` | double | false | false | In-state tuition and fees. **Display-only / receipt field** per `roi-formula-cost-of-attendance.md` §3 ("For receipts"). It is *not* a driver of `net_price_annual` (net price already nets grants/scholarships off the cost of attendance — it is the authoritative cost input to ROI). Tuition is one component of COA, not the whole thing; surfacing it on receipts helps the student understand the breakdown but doesn't drive any stat, boss fight, or ROI score. |
| 21 | `tuitionfee_out` | double | false | false | Out-of-state tuition and fees. Same role as `tuitionfee_in` — display/receipt only. Not a driver of any stat or boss fight. |
| 22 | `roomboard_on` | double | false | false | On-campus room and board. Display/receipt only ("For receipts" per ROI spec §3). Already rolled into `costt4_a/costt4_p` (COA is inclusive of room & board per the spec table: "Yes — tuition + fees + books + room & board + living expenses"). Surfacing it separately helps the receipt transparency moment ("your $22,800 COA breaks down as $10K tuition + $12K room & board + ...") but does not drive downstream formulas. |
| 23 | `roomboard_off` | double | false | false | Off-campus room and board. Same role as `roomboard_on`. Already rolled into COA. Display/receipt only. |
| 24 | `booksupply` | double | false | false | Books and supplies estimate. Display/receipt only. Already rolled into COA. Not a driver of any stat or boss fight. |
| 25 | `ingested_at` | timestamp | false | false | Pipeline metadata — Bronze promotion timestamp. Identical across all rows in a single batch load. Useful for freshness tracking but not a business-decision input. Aligned with the sibling `raw-college-scorecard.yaml` contract where `ingested_at` is also non-CDE. |
| 26 | `source_url` | string | false | false | Pipeline metadata — the public College Scorecard download URL. Provenance tracking only. No secrets-hygiene concern here (this URL is an unauthenticated public endpoint — unlike the BEA RPP case, there is no API key to redact). |
| 27 | `source_method` | string | false | false | Pipeline metadata — literal string `"bulk_csv_download"`. Provenance tracking only. |
| 28 | `load_date` | date | false | false | Pipeline metadata — date of load. Not a business-decision input. Aligned with sibling contract. |

---

## Forward Note: Silver — `base.college_scorecard_institution`

The Silver contract will be tagged separately when @doc-generator produces `governance/data-contracts/base-college-scorecard-institution.yaml`. Per the no-propagation rule, the flags below are *recommendations* for that downstream tagging pass, not binding decisions on this Bronze contract. Each derived column's CDE-worthiness can be re-assessed against the Silver schema's actual downstream usage at that time.

**Expected Silver CDEs** (preview, based on the Silver schema in §Zone 2 of the spec):

| Silver Column | Recommended `is_cde` | Why |
|---------------|----------------------|-----|
| `record_id` | **true** | Deterministic grain hash (prefix `csi`). Dedup grain for Silver. |
| `unitid` | **true** | Join key to Bronze lineage and to Gold `consumable.career_outcomes` LEFT JOIN. |
| `institution_name` | false | Display label (already non-CDE at Bronze as `instnm`). |
| `state_abbr` | false | Same reasoning as `stabbr` at Bronze. Display/filter only. |
| `institution_control` | **true** | String-mapped form of `control`. Still the multiplexer (already used up in the Silver transform, but carried through for display/filter downstream — Gold receipts show "Public" / "Private nonprofit" / "Private for-profit"). Also driver of any future control-type segmentation queries. |
| `cost_of_attendance_annual` | **true** | The unified COA (`COALESCE(costt4_a, costt4_p)`). Receipt line + input to `cost_of_attendance_4yr`. BT-110. |
| `net_price_annual` | **true** | **The ROI denominator.** Most critical column in the Silver table. BT-111. |
| `net_price_4yr` | **true** | `net_price_annual × 4` — directly consumed by the ROI formula `earnings / (net_price × 4 × loan_pct)`. Pre-materialized for Gold query performance. |
| `cost_of_attendance_4yr` | **true** | `cost_of_attendance_annual × 4` — receipt line and comparison baseline. |
| `net_price_q1..q5` | **true** (all five) | Income-quintile net price, unified. Drives income-quintile ROI stretch goals. BT-112. Aid-monotonicity DQ invariant. |
| `tuition_in_state` | false | Display/receipt only. |
| `tuition_out_of_state` | false | Display/receipt only. |
| `room_board_on_campus` | false | Display/receipt only. Rolled into COA. |
| `room_board_off_campus` | false | Display/receipt only. Rolled into COA. |
| `books_supplies` | false | Display/receipt only. Rolled into COA. |
| `source_load_date` | false | Pipeline metadata. |
| `ingested_at` | false | Pipeline metadata. |

**Silver PII:** None — inherits the Level 1 — Public classification from Bronze per the PII scan.

---

## Tag List for Data Contract (for @doc-generator to embed)

When `governance/data-contracts/raw-college-scorecard-institution.yaml` is generated, embed the following column-level flags. Mirrors the sibling `raw-college-scorecard.yaml` structure.

```yaml
# Fragment — to be merged by @doc-generator into the raw-college-scorecard-institution.yaml contract
columns:
  - name: unitid
    type: long
    required: true
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: >
      Sole join key to downstream consumers (base.college_scorecard_institution,
      base.college_scorecard, and the Gold LEFT JOIN into consumable.career_outcomes).
      Also dedup grain for Bronze ingest. IPEDS authoritative identifier — auto-approved
      per governance/domain-context.md. Aligned with sibling raw-college-scorecard.yaml
      where unitid is CDE.
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Institution IPEDS ID (6-digit). Grain key. Joins to raw.college_scorecard and consumable.career_outcomes on the same UNITID."

  - name: instnm
    type: string
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: >
      Institution name (display only). Not unique — 10 names map to multiple UNITIDs
      (multi-campus systems). UNITID is the authoritative identifier.

  - name: stabbr
    type: string
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "2-letter state abbreviation. Institution-level geographic descriptor. Not a join key in this pipeline."

  - name: control
    type: int
    required: true
    business_term: BT-CSI-CONTROL
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: >
      Multiplexer that selects NPT4_PUB vs NPT4_PRIV (and the ten quintile columns)
      in the Silver transform. A mis-tagged control value routes the ROI denominator
      to the wrong source column, producing silently wrong ROI. DQ rule enforces
      control IN (1,2,3). IPEDS external standard.
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Institution control: 1=Public, 2=Private nonprofit, 3=Private for-profit."

  - name: preddeg
    type: int
    required: true
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: >
      Ingest filter input — the scope of this table (4-year bachelor's-granting institutions)
      is defined by PREDDEG=3 OR ICLEVEL=1. Validates filter correctness. IPEDS external standard.
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Predominant degree awarded. Value 3 = Bachelor's. Used as the primary scope filter (OR ICLEVEL=1)."

  - name: costt4_a
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-110
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: >
      Primary source of unified cost_of_attendance_annual in Silver (COALESCE(costt4_a, costt4_p)).
      Feeds the sticker-price receipt line and cost_of_attendance_4yr derivation.
      P0 range check $5,000-$100,000. BT-110 (Cost of Attendance).
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average cost of attendance, academic-year programs. Includes tuition, fees, books, room & board, living expenses."

  - name: costt4_p
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-110
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: >
      Fallback source of unified cost_of_attendance_annual in Silver (used when costt4_a is null,
      for program-year institutions). BT-110 (Cost of Attendance).
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average cost of attendance, program-year programs. Same inclusion scope as costt4_a."

  - name: npt4_pub
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-111
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: >
      CRITICAL CDE. Direct source of net_price_annual for public institutions (control=1) —
      the ROI denominator input in the new formula earnings / (net_price * 4 * loan_pct).
      Every ROI score and Fight Student Loans outcome at a public school traces back through
      Silver to this column. P0 range check $0-$60,000. DQ rule: >=80% non-null where control=1.
      BT-111 (Net Price).
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average net price for public institutions (COA minus grants/scholarships). ROI denominator for control=1."

  - name: npt4_priv
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-111
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: >
      CRITICAL CDE. Direct source of net_price_annual for private institutions (control IN (2,3)).
      Same role as npt4_pub for the private branch of the ROI formula. P0 range check $0-$80,000.
      DQ rule: >=80% non-null where control=2. BT-111 (Net Price).
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average net price for private institutions (nonprofit or for-profit). ROI denominator for control IN (2,3)."

  - name: npt41_pub
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-112
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Drives net_price_q1 in Silver for public institutions ($0-$30K income bracket). Feeds income-quintile ROI. BT-112."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average net price for $0-$30K family income, public institutions."

  - name: npt42_pub
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-112
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Drives net_price_q2 in Silver for public institutions ($30K-$48K income bracket). Feeds income-quintile ROI. BT-112."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average net price for $30K-$48K family income, public institutions."

  - name: npt43_pub
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-112
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Drives net_price_q3 in Silver for public institutions ($48K-$75K income bracket — median bracket). BT-112."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average net price for $48K-$75K family income, public institutions."

  - name: npt44_pub
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-112
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Drives net_price_q4 in Silver for public institutions ($75K-$110K income bracket). BT-112."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average net price for $75K-$110K family income, public institutions."

  - name: npt45_pub
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-112
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: >
      Drives net_price_q5 in Silver for public institutions ($110K+ income bracket).
      Part of the aid-monotonicity DQ invariant (net_price_q1 <= net_price_q5). BT-112.
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average net price for $110K+ family income, public institutions."

  - name: npt41_priv
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-112
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Drives net_price_q1 in Silver for private institutions ($0-$30K income bracket). BT-112."
    is_pii: false

    required: false

    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Drives net_price_q4 in Silver for private institutions ($75K-$110K income bracket). BT-112."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average net price for $75K-$110K family income, private institutions."

  - name: npt45_priv
    type: double
    required: false
    business_term: BT-112
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: >
      Drives net_price_q5 in Silver for private institutions ($110K+ income bracket).
      Part of the aid-monotonicity DQ invariant. BT-112.
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Average net price for $110K+ family income, private institutions."

  - name: tuitionfee_in
    type: double
    required: false

    cde_rationale: ""

    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "In-state tuition and fees. Display/receipt only — already rolled into COA."

  - name: roomboard_on
    type: double

    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "On-campus room and board. Display/receipt only — already rolled into COA."

  - name: roomboard_off
    type: double
    required: false
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Off-campus room and board. Display/receipt only — already rolled into COA."

  - name: booksupply
    type: double
    required: false
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Books and supplies estimate. Display/receipt only — already rolled into COA."

  - name: ingested_at
    type: timestamp
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Pipeline metadata — Bronze promotion timestamp."

  - name: source_url
    type: string
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Pipeline metadata — public College Scorecard download URL. No secrets-hygiene concern (unauthenticated public endpoint)."

  - name: source_method
    type: string
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Pipeline metadata — literal 'bulk_csv_download'."

  - name: load_date
    type: date
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Pipeline metadata — date of load."
```

---

## Summary

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Columns evaluated (Bronze) | 28 |
| Columns flagged CDE | **17** (1 grain key + 2 scope/multiplexer + 2 COA sources + 2 primary net price + 10 quintile net prices) |
| Columns flagged PII | **0** |
| Columns not flagged | 11 (2 display labels + 5 cost components for receipts + 4 pipeline metadata) |
| Regulatory frameworks triggered | None — public federal data |
| Sensitivity classification | `public` across all 28 columns (per pii-scan) |
| CDE density | 60.7% (17/28) — higher than typical Bronze tables because the spec wires nearly every substantive source column directly into the new ROI formula or a named Silver derivation |

### CDE-density commentary

The 60.7% CDE density here is notably higher than the 37.5% density on the sibling `raw-college-scorecard` Bronze contract (6/16 flagged). The explanation is not governance inflation — it is that **this ingest was explicitly scoped to fields that drive a new business stat.** The spec lists ~15 source fields precisely because each was selected to feed either `net_price_annual`, `cost_of_attendance_annual`, or the receipt breakdown. The non-CDE residue (tuition, room-board, books) is the display/receipt surface area. There are essentially no "just descriptive" columns in this ingest the way there are in the program-level Scorecard file (`cipdesc`, `creddesc`, `ipedscount1`, `ipedscount2`).

### Downstream reminder

CDE flags do not propagate. When this agent re-runs on:

- `silver-base-college-scorecard-institution` — the derived columns (`net_price_annual`, `cost_of_attendance_annual`, `net_price_4yr`, `cost_of_attendance_4yr`, `net_price_q1..q5`, `institution_control`) will be re-evaluated on their own merits in the Silver zone per the Forward Note above.
- `gold-career-outcomes-institution-enrichment` (or whichever spec lands the Gold LEFT JOIN) — the enrichment columns added to `consumable.career_outcomes` (`net_price_annual`, `cost_of_attendance_annual`, `net_price_4yr`, `institution_control`, `tuition_in_state`, `tuition_out_of_state`, `room_board_on_campus`) will be re-evaluated against Gold-zone consumer patterns (MCP tools, stat engine, Fight Student Loans, receipts, loan-slider UI). Expect the Gold tagging to flag `net_price_annual` and `cost_of_attendance_annual` as the two highest-criticality CDEs, with `institution_control` promoted as well (Gold receipts and the MCP tool response both display the control-type string).
